commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs human review for the live Feishu proof gate; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to apply.; Cleared: The diff only changes Feishu dispatcher control flow and colocated tests, with no dependency, workflow, credential, or supply-chain surface added. (sha=433f4c5cbe16e5e375824ef17cdf2eb29c9ac505)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes the Feishu reply dispatcher so tool-result payloads bypass the active streaming-card early return and adds dispatcher tests for text and media tool results.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +63. Total +66 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main returns from the active streaming-card branch before text-only tool results reach the normal send path. I did not establish live Feishu reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs human review for the live Feishu proof gate; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to apply.

target.title: fix: resolve issue #79748: deliver Feishu tool results during streaming
target.body:
## Summary
- Fix message blocking caused by browser-related operations in Feishu group chat channels. Tool results (including browser tool outputs) are now delivered as separate messages even when a streaming card is active.
- Previously, when `info.kind === "tool"`, the dispatcher returned early in the streaming path, silently dropping tool result text. Now only non-tool messages take the early return, while tool results fall through to be delivered as separate cards.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Feishu group chat silently drops tool result messages (browser outputs, command results) when a streaming card is active. The reply dispatcher's early-return in the streaming path catches all messages including tool results, causing them to disappear.

**Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node.js v22.22.0. The fix is pure JavaScript control-flow in the Feishu reply dispatcher and is platform-independent.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch**:
```bash
cd openclaw-79748
node proof_repro.mjs
```
The script simulates the reply-dispatcher branching logic: activates a streaming card, then sends a tool result and a non-tool message to verify that tool results are no longer dropped while non-tool messages still take the early return.

**Evidence after fix**:
```
=== Feishu streaming tool-result drop fix (#79748) ===
Timestamp : 2026-05-20T03:42:12.795Z
Node.js   : v22.22.0
Platform  : linux x64

--- Scenario: streaming card active, tool result arrives ---
BEFORE fix: delivered=0, tool result DROPPED
AFTER  fix: delivered=1, tool result DELIVERED

--- Scenario: streaming card active, non-tool message ---
Non-tool message takes early return: YES (correct)

=== Result ===
Tool result no longer dropped: PASS
Non-tool path unchanged      : PASS
Overall                      : PASS
```

**Observed result after fix**: Before fix: tool result with kind=tool is silently dropped (delivered=0) when streaming card is active. After fix: tool result falls through and is delivered as a separate card (delivered=1). Non-tool messages still take the early return path unchanged.

**What was not tested**: Live Feishu API integration with real bot credentials. Multi-turn streaming with interleaved tool results.

Fixes #79748
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
